mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rate levels increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and contraction in consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer demand without monetary policy tightening pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in >55% of occurrences despite favorable macro conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side capacity utilization and corporate output expansion, generating positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance with p-value greater than 0.10 during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 320.0 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, suppressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of periods when CPIAUCSL remains above 320.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market strength, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in >50% of observed months.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product levels reflect sustained aggregate demand expansion, directly supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all 3-year rolling windows during expansionary phases.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing and industrial output, triggering recession fears and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances following two consecutive monthly declines.
